name: guaraná
mechanism: Mechanistic hypotheses center on caffeine and methylxanthine-mediated adenosine antagonism plus possible polyphenol contributions.
effects: Meta-analysis reports modest improvements in selected cognitive-performance domains.
Clinical effects are context-dependent and should not be generalized to all formulations.
contraindications: Use caution in people with arrhythmia risk, uncontrolled hypertension, panic disorder, or severe insomnia.
safetyNotes: Caffeine-related adverse effects (for example insomnia, palpitations, anxiety) remain a practical safety concern.
description: Guarana (Paullinia cupana) is a caffeine-rich Amazonian plant used in beverages and supplements, with modern clinical syntheses focused on cognition and fatigue.
dosage: Clinical dosing varies by caffeine-equivalent content and standardized extract composition.
duration: No direct duration data. Contextual inference: Used in ritual, dreaming or folk medicine.. No direct mechanismofaction data. interactions: Potential additive stimulation with caffeine-containing products, sympathomimetics, and other CNS stimulants.
preparation: Prepared as seeds/powder in traditional beverages and as standardized extracts in supplements.
region: Indigenous to the Amazon basin, especially Brazil, with global commercial distribution.
traditionalUse: Traditional Amazonian use includes stimulant and endurance contexts.
summary: Systematic-review and meta-analytic evidence suggests guarana can improve some cognitive-performance outcomes, but effects vary by dose, comparator, and caffeine background.

researchEnrichment.evidenceSummary: Indexability remediation update for guarana using curated registry sources and claim-level provenance.

supportedUses.claim: Systematic-review and meta-analytic evidence suggests guarana can improve some cognitive-performance outcomes, but effects vary by dose, comparator, and caffeine background.

unsupportedOrUnclearUses.claim: Evidence remains limited by study design heterogeneity and/or sparse direct human trials for this exact herb preparation.

mechanisms.claim: Mechanistic hypotheses center on caffeine and methylxanthine-mediated adenosine antagonism plus possible polyphenol contributions.
mechanisms.uncertaintyNote: Mechanistic findings should not be interpreted as proven clinical efficacy.

interactions.claim: Potential additive stimulation with caffeine-containing products, sympathomimetics, and other CNS stimulants.

contraindications.claim: Use caution in people with arrhythmia risk, uncontrolled hypertension, panic disorder, or severe insomnia.

adverseEffects.claim: Caffeine-related adverse effects (for example insomnia, palpitations, anxiety) remain a practical safety concern.

dosageContextNotes.claim: Clinical dosing varies by caffeine-equivalent content and standardized extract composition.

researchGaps.claim: High-quality, formulation-specific human trials and longer-term safety follow-up are still needed.
